wikilite: Retrieve, Parse, and Analyse Wikipedia Article History and Citations

Source: R/wikilite-package.R
wikilite-package.Rd

**wikilite** is a toolkit for mining Wikipedia article revision history and citations via the public MediaWiki API. Core capabilities:

History retrieval

Full or partial revision histories for one or many articles via get_article_full_history_table, get_article_initial_table, get_article_most_recent_table.

Category navigation

get_pagename_in_cat, get_subcat_table, get_subcat_with_depth and related helpers.

Citation extraction

Built-in regular expressions for DOIs, ISBNs, PMIDs, URLs, and all Citation Style 1 templates, accessible via get_regex_citations_in_wiki_table or extract_citations_regexp.

Citation parsing

parse_article_ALL_citations parses every CS1 template into a tidy long data frame; counting helpers (get_doi_count, get_refCount, …) provide fast scalar summaries.

Quality metrics

get_sci_score (proportion of journal citations).

Annotation

annotate_doi_list_europmc, annotate_doi_list_cross_ref, annotate_doi_list_altmetrics, annotate_isbn_google, annotate_isbn_openlib.

Revert trends

get_revert_counts retrieves the count of revert-tagged edits across all Wikipedia articles for a given time window.

Visualisation

Timelines, edit-activity plots, and citation distribution charts built on ggplot2.

Built-in regular expressions

The package-level environment pkg.env contains all pre-built patterns. Access via pkg.env$doi_regexp, pkg.env$regexp_list, etc.

See also

Useful links:

  • https://github.com/jsobel1/wikilite

  • Report bugs at https://github.com/jsobel1/wikilite/issues
